job_title: Executive Assistant / Administrative Assistant (Financial Services Industry Experience Required)
strengths: Proficient with MS Office and Google Workspace
weaknesses: No explicit onsite New York 10172 mention
No direct mention of receptionist greeting visitors
No explicit mention of expense report software (e.g., Concur)
No formal certification in administrative tools
Limited recent long-term tenure (current role started Jun 2024)
improvement_bullets: Manage front-office operations by greeting visitors, managing reception, and ensuring a professional atmosphere for clients and executives in the New York, NY area.
Streamline executive expense reporting by utilizing Concur and SAP to reconcile monthly expenditures, ensuring 100% policy compliance.
Provide dedicated on-site administrative support at company headquarters in New York, consistently ensuring operational continuity and presence for all team members.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Executive Assistant (Financial Services Industry Experience Required) opportunity with Sharp Decisions. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's reputation for operational excellence and its commitment to supporting high-level executive teams in the fast-paced New York market. What interests me about working for Sharp Decisions is its forward-thinking approach to organizational efficiency, which aligns perfectly with my professional philosophy. I believe my expertise in complex calendar management and my background in high-level executive operations would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a proactive and meticulous mindset. In fact, at Direct Clean Solutions, I recognized that our executive travel and meeting scheduling lacked a unified system, so I implemented a new tracking protocol using advanced CRM and spreadsheet tools. This resulted in a 25% reduction in scheduling conflicts and saved the executive team an average of five hours per week in coordination time, allowing them to focus entirely on business development. Ultimately, I know that the Executive Assistant role at Sharp Decisions would allow me to bring my mastery of executive calendar management to the daily scheduling needs of your leadership team. Additionally, my role as an Executive Administrative Assistant at Direct Clean Solutions enabled me to develop my comprehensive travel and expense reporting skills. I successfully managed international and domestic travel itineraries, often coordinating complex visa requirements and reconciling travel expenses using industry-standard software to maintain audit readiness. This experience has prepared me to hit the ground running with Sharp Decisions' expense reporting and travel coordination requirements. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my proactive problem-solving skills in working on general office duties and internal communications at Sharp Decisions. I know that my background in supporting C-suite executives and my proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Millieann Velez
